fields.name: Entry No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the entry number of the lines.
The program fills this field in automatically, and the program uses this field used internally for managing the Where-Used Line table.

fields.name: Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item that the base item or production BOM is assigned to.

fields.name: Version Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the version code of the production BOM that the item or production BOM component is assigned to.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the description of the item to which the item or production BOM component is assigned.

fields.name: Quantity Needed
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the item or the production BOM component that is needed for the assigned item.

fields.name: Level Code
fields.tooltip:
Shows the level of the production BOM that this item is used in.
If an item goes directly into the production BOM, then level 1 is displayed here. If, however, the item is also used in another production BOM, level 2 is displayed here.
For example, a bicycle is produced. In level 1, all the parts are displayed which will be required for the final assembly. An example of such a part is a front wheel. However, for the production of this front wheel, one rim, 50 spokes and other components are needed. Therefore, the rim is displayed in level 2 because it is a component that is used in the front wheel.

fields.name: Production BOM No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the production BOM. The number identifies the production BOM that the production of this item is based on.
To see the production BOM numbers in the Production BOM table, click the field.
